tier_intro: The deepest layer. Not a clinical assessment — a practical personality map that tells AI how to actually treat you as a human. What motivates you, how you handle stress, how you relate to risk and change. Real questions that get at real answers. 6-8 minutes.

tier_complete.headline: Full personality profile built.
tier_complete.body: This is the layer most tools skip entirely. AI now has a complete picture of you — not just preferences, but how you think, work, and navigate the world. Your profile is ready to export. One profile, any AI platform.

questions.question: Friday night, zero plans, zero obligations. Your ideal scenario?

options.label: Call someone, go out, be around people — recharge through connection
One or two close people — meaningful conversation over noise
Alone time — solo project, show, book. Complete recharge.
Depends entirely on how the week went

questions.meta_profiling: Classic extraversion scenario — response time and length give secondary signals. Also note: if they said 'depends on week' — this is a self-aware introvert or ambivert.
questions.why_this_matters: Social energy preferences affect how AI engages — does it create space or fill it?

questions.question: After a full day of socializing — meeting people, presentations, group work — you feel...

options.label: Energized — people fuel me
Satisfied but ready for quiet — good day, now I need to recharge alone
Drained — even if it went well, I need solo time to recover
Depends entirely on who I was with

questions.meta_profiling: Social battery combined with extraversion from t4_q01 gives full introvert/extrovert picture. Inconsistency between the two = situational extraversion (ambivert or context-sensitive).
questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates how much social framing and collaboration it suggests — and when to leave you alone.

questions.question: What actually motivates you to push through hard work? Your honest answer:

options.label: Building something that didn't exist before — creation itself
Freedom — financial, time, or location freedom as the outcome
Recognition — being seen and respected for what you've done
Impact — knowing it matters to real people
Mastery — getting really, genuinely good at something
Winning — being better than the alternative or the competition

questions.meta_profiling: Core motivation is a high-signal dimension that should inform ALL AI framing. Recognition-motivated people respond to different language than autonomy-motivated ones.
questions.why_this_matters: AI frames recommendations, challenges, and progress in terms of what actually moves you.

questions.question: If you could keep only 3 things in your life — which 3? (rank or pick)
questions.instruction: Pick your top 3 — order them if you can

options.label: Close relationships — family, real friends
Freedom — ability to do what you want, when you want
Meaningful work — building something that matters
Health — physical and mental wellbeing
Financial security — not worrying about money
Growth — constantly learning and improving

questions.meta_profiling: Values hierarchy is the deepest profiling dimension. Inconsistencies with stated motivations (t4_q03) are often the most interesting signals — what people say vs what they choose.
questions.why_this_matters: AI understands what's actually at stake for you — not just what you're working on.

questions.question: An opportunity lands in front of you — potentially big, but genuinely uncertain. You:

options.label: Jump in — uncertainty is part of the opportunity
Calculate the downside first — if I can survive the worst case, I'm in
Get more information before deciding — insufficient data
Probably pass unless someone I trust validates it strongly

questions.meta_profiling: Risk tolerance cross-references with decision style from Tier 2. Consistent picture = note as stable trait. Contradictions = note for context-dependency.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to present options with upside-first or risk-first framing.

questions.question: You and someone you work with have a genuine disagreement. You tend to:

options.label: Address it directly and immediately — let's settle this
Avoid it if possible — not worth the energy unless it really matters
Process internally first, then address when I have a clear head
Look for the middle ground proactively — meet them halfway

questions.meta_profiling: Conflict style affects how AI handles disagreements with user. Avoidant = AI should soften challenges. Direct = AI can disagree more directly.
questions.why_this_matters: Sets the tone for how AI disagrees with you — and how persistent it should be when you push back.

questions.question: Under genuine stress — not mild inconvenience, but real pressure — what do people around you notice?

options.label: I go quiet — process internally, want to be left alone
Short-tempered — I snap, then apologize after
Lock in on the problem — tunnel vision until it's solved
Talk excessively — I need to verbally process to calm down
Freeze up — hard to make decisions or take action

questions.meta_profiling: Stress response is a critical safety dimension — affects how AI should behave when user signals high pressure. Irritable pattern = AI should reduce friction, not ask questions. Freeze = AI should simplify and give one clear action.
questions.why_this_matters: When you're under pressure, AI adapts its approach to match how you actually handle stress.

questions.question: Deadline tomorrow, the thing you need isn't ready, and it's your call. First thought?

options.label: What can I actually do right now? I'm already making a list.
Communicate proactively — tell the stakeholder now, not at deadline
Push harder — I'll pull an all-nighter if I have to
Brief spiral of 'this is bad', then I find my feet and act

questions.meta_profiling: Stress coping style is the activation pattern under pressure. Spiral-then-act = AI should give one clear action to break the spiral. Transparent = AI should help draft communications early.
questions.why_this_matters: AI provides the right kind of support during crunch — action lists, communications, or just helping you break the spiral.

questions.question: Your company reorganizes everything — new team, new tools, new processes. You feel:

options.label: Energized — change is interesting, fresh start
Cautiously open — depends on what's changing and why
Unsettled initially but I adapt — I just need to find the new pattern
Resistant — I prefer the known even if it's imperfect

questions.why_this_matters: AI handles tool changes, workflow suggestions, and pivots — does it push the new thing hard or help you transition gently?

questions.question: Your boss gives you an instruction you genuinely disagree with. You:

options.label: Voice my disagreement once, clearly — then do it if they hold the call
Push back hard — I can't execute something I think is wrong without a fight
Do it — they have context I might not, and hierarchy exists for a reason
Nod and find a way to do it that I think is better

questions.meta_profiling: Authority orientation reveals relationship with hierarchical structures. High autonomy + challenging = AI should not be authoritative; should present options. Deferential = AI can be more directive.
questions.why_this_matters: Shapes whether AI acts as an equal collaborator or a helpful assistant.

questions.question: Trolley problem, slightly less deadly: You can do something that helps 10 people but hurts 1 person who doesn't deserve it. You...

options.label: Do it — the math is clear, 10 > 1
Don't do it — some things are wrong regardless of outcome
Depends on who the 1 is and how much they're hurt
Find a way to help the 10 without hurting the 1 — accept neither option

questions.meta_profiling: Ethical framework is not about judging — it's about how AI should frame trade-off decisions. Consequentialist = present outcomes. Rule-based = present principles. Problem-solver = AI should always look for third options.
questions.why_this_matters: How AI presents decisions with trade-offs — and whether it looks for alternatives or just gives you the choice.

questions.question: You're on hold for 20 minutes. The task will take 2 minutes when someone answers. You:

options.label: Stay on the line, focused — I need this resolved
Put on hold music and do something else
Hang up after 5 minutes and find a faster way
Irrationally irritated by the entire situation out of proportion to what it is

questions.meta_profiling: Patience level cross-references with verbosity preference and feedback style. Very low patience + minimal verbosity = user who will not re-engage AI that wastes their time even once.
questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates its own response time, interruptions, and willingness to take roundabout paths.

questions.question: Plans fall through at the last minute — something you were looking forward to. Your first thought?

options.label: OK, what else can I do with this time? Immediately looking for alternatives.
Disappointed, but I regroup quickly — it's fine
Genuinely frustrated — I sit with that for a bit, then move on
Honestly expected it — things usually do fall through

questions.meta_profiling: Optimism baseline shapes how AI frames uncertainty and setbacks. Low optimism = AI should not overpromise or be relentlessly positive.
questions.why_this_matters: AI matches its emotional tone to your baseline — doesn't try to artificially cheer up a realistic person.

questions.question: Where does that expectation come from?

options.label: I'd rather be pleasantly surprised than disappointed — it's self-protection
Experience — things genuinely do fall through more often than not for me
I'm just calibrated — not cynical, just realistic about probabilities

questions.meta_profiling: The framing they choose reveals self-narrative around pessimism — protective vs experiential vs calibrated each require different AI handling.
questions.why_this_matters: Helps AI calibrate whether to offer encouragement, validation, or just match your realistic tone.

questions.question: New colleague starts Monday. Before you've met them, your default assumption about them?

options.label: They're probably competent and well-intentioned — trust until disproven
Neutral — I'll form a view once I see them work
Cautious — trust is earned, not assumed
Depends on how they were hired and who they are

questions.meta_profiling: Trust default affects how AI should frame its own relationship with the user. High trust = AI can be direct and proactive. Low trust = AI should show its reasoning, not just its conclusions.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to lead with conclusions or always show its work so you can verify.

questions.question: You just shipped something. It works, people are happy, but you KNOW there's a subtle issue nobody else will notice. You...

options.label: Fix it immediately — I can't leave it. It'll eat at me until I do.
Note it for later — I care, but I can live with it for now
Move on — it works, nobody notices, there's more important stuff to do
Depends what it is — a typo I can live with, a logic flaw I can't

questions.meta_profiling: High perfectionism + incomplete completion (t3_q16 perfectionist) = reinforced pattern. The scenario reveals actual behavior vs stated preference — someone who says 'I'm pragmatic' but admits they'd fix it = hidden perfectionist.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to push you to ship sooner or help you find the right 'done' threshold.

questions.question: Is your perfectionism usually productive (drives quality) or does it sometimes hold you back?

options.label: Mostly productive — it's a strength I use consciously
Mixed — productive on things I care about, paralyzing on others
Often blocks me — I know it's a problem

questions.question: Something unexpected and wonderful happens — you got the deal, your project got featured, genuine praise from someone you respect. Your first instinct?

options.label: Share it immediately — call someone, post it, tell the world
Feel it internally — deep satisfaction. Maybe tell one person later.
Mild suspicion — when does the other shoe drop?
Acknowledge it, then immediately move to what's next

questions.meta_profiling: Cautious optimist response combined with high conscientiousness from other signals = anxiety-adjacent trait. Note for gentle challenge handling.
questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates celebration and acknowledgment to how you actually receive good news.

questions.question: A friend describes you in 3 words to someone who's never met you. They'd most likely say...
questions.instruction: Pick the 3 that ring most true

options.label: Direct
Creative
Analytical
Reliable
Intense
Curious
Independent
Empathetic
Ambitious
Chaotic (in a good way)

questions.meta_profiling: Self-perception is a meta-signal. Cross-reference with how they actually behaved throughout the profiling — answers that contradict self-perception are the most interesting. Note incongruence without flagging it.
questions.why_this_matters: AI adjusts its relational style to match how you see yourself — not project a different identity onto you.

questions.question: You've finished a piece of work you're genuinely proud of. Nobody's seen it yet. You...

options.label: Already know it's good — I don't need anyone to tell me
Feel good about it, but it'd be nice if someone confirmed
Need to show one person I trust before I feel confident sharing widely
Won't feel sure about it until someone else reacts — my own judgment isn't enough

questions.meta_profiling: High validation need = AI should proactively affirm direction and signal agreement. Low = AI should not be sycophantic, just direct. Note: response time here is a secondary signal — fast pick = confident in self-knowledge.
questions.why_this_matters: AI calibrates how much it confirms, affirms, and checks in — vs just executes without commentary.

questions.question: You're deciding between two things — one you know is right, one that sounds exciting right now. You tend to:

options.label: Long-term wins — I can delay gratification pretty well
About 50/50 — depends on how exciting the short-term option is
Short-term often wins — I'm working on this
I rationalize the short-term choice as actually being better long-term

questions.meta_profiling: Impulsivity indicator cross-references with spending patterns implied in Tier 7 (life context). Low impulse control = AI should add 'sleep on it' friction to big decisions.
questions.why_this_matters: AI knows whether to present options with equal weight or add a pause before big decisions.

questions.question: In which areas does this show up most?

options.label: Purchases — I buy things I didn't plan on
New projects — I start things before finishing what's open
Decisions — I commit too fast
Food and health habits

questions.meta_profiling: project_hopping = strong ADHD-associated pattern. Cross-reference with completion_pattern from Tier 3.

questions.question: Last one. If AI could do one thing differently that almost every AI gets wrong for you — what would it be?
questions.placeholder: Your honest answer here — this goes directly into your profile
questions.meta_profiling: This is the highest-value open text question in the entire bank. Response length, content, and tone carry enormous signal. Note: very short answer = either they're satisfied or they're low-verbosity. Very detailed = high investment, pain points are real. Save verbatim.
questions.why_this_matters: Your words, verbatim. The one thing your AI profile is built around fixing for you.
